caption: This educational presentation on "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" focuses on "Heat Exchanger Effectiveness." Each frame displays a schematic of a heat exchanger with fluid inputs and outputs, labeled "Fluid$_A$, in, hot", "Fluid$_A$, out, cold", "Fluid$_B$, out, hot", and "Fluid$_B$, in, cold". To the right, the text states, "The actual heat exchange is" followed by equations for $\dot{Q}_{act}$. Below the schematic, a graph plots temperature ("Temp.") against "Length, x", showing temperature curves for fluids A and B and a symbol $\varepsilon$. The bottom of each frame credits "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay" and includes an "NPTEL" logo. Between frames, the colored visualization of the heat exchanger changes, shifting from a blue-red gradient to a multi-colored spectrum and then to vertical stripes. In the second row, additional text appears on the right: "The maximum possible heat exchange is" followed by an equation for $\dot{Q}_{max}$.
